text: Video Idea: 100-200 people survive inside a real abandoned/crafted city for 7 days during a zombie apocalypse. Start with 5 hidden zombies and everyone wears a wristband showing if they’re human, infected, or vaccinated. Zombies infect people by tagging them, but vaccines are hidden around the city and can turn zombies back into survivors. Every hour survivor bracelets ping their location so zombies can hunt them down. There are food/supply drops and daily high-risk challenges for money, weapons, clues, or vaccines. On Day 7, an extraction gate opens and everyone left alive has to make it there before getting infected. Would be like a real-life survival movie mixed with strategy and chaos

author: @TeamZeroPercent
text:
Dope video 😎

It was a long journey for us, but we’re very happy that we can call it a victory! Thank you to everyone who joined in the effort; it was a lot of fun! We’re still working on Mario Maker 2 of course, and there’s a lot of forgotten (and probably fairly easy) levels to get through still, so come join in! We’ve already cleared all the levels uploaded between 2019 and 2022, and 2023 is likely to fall sometime this month. There’s a lot of levels from 2024 and 2025 still, so we need all the help we can get!
video_title: The Story Of Mario Maker's Impossible Levels

author: @KyrosQuickfist
text:
"Welcome Extreme" was actually a remake of the most viral Japanese level made on the FIRST DAY of Mario Maker. The creator of this remake did speedruns of it so much he made a 3 lap version with a tight timer and then uploaded it. I kinda wish this bit of lore made it into the video because it is a special level in its own way.

It's great seeing Budoko get his own section, he deserves it for doing those levels 😂. He beat a level by the creator of Trials of Death called "Trials of Time", a gimmick that paused the timer constantly making a single attempt require more than 2 hours of game time to clear and wasn't automatic.
video_title: The Story Of Mario Maker's Impossible Levels

author: @RidleyUwO
text: I said this before and I’ll say it again: Trimming The Herbs is like the final boss in a JRPG where everyone is telling you you can’t kill him and you got to seal him to make it the next generation’s problem. But instead of just going about the fight, you jump through every hoop possible and fight all the optional super bosses, making the final fight significantly harder, all so you can get the true ending and kill the boss. It’s just so peak…

author: @dukepebbles
text: I keep thinking about the mostly unknown people who made all these incredibly difficult levels. Salt talks about some of the best players in the world grinding for months on this, but each of these levels was beaten before, often by someone who just made it, tried it countless times, beat it, and then vanished. In a way that feels even more impressive than the people who've played Mario Maker for years grinding on them.
video_title: The Story Of Mario Maker's Impossible Levels

author: @SpriteGuard
text: One thing not mentioned is that TTH was actually debuted as part of a Mario Maker 10 second level design competition. There were a bunch of categories, and one of them was "hardest" and they basically admitted that category was rigged -- TTH is 12 seconds, and was made by one of the people running the competition. They really made that category just to show off the level, and people *still* didn't really pick up on it.
video_title: The Story Of Mario Maker's Impossible Levels

author: @_iiRose
text:
This particular Resident Evil game will always have a special place in my heart. When I was little, my sister used to play it while she was undergoing cancer treatment, and I would sit beside her reading the magazines and guides because I was too young to play myself. Even during such a difficult time, we created memories filled with laughter and happiness.

She has since passed away, but every time I see this game, I think of her and the moments we shared together. Those memories will stay with me forever🤍

I also want to thank the Resident Evil team for always respecting their players and continuously striving to deliver the best possible experience. Their passion and dedication have created unforgettable memories for so many people, including me.

Thank you, Resident Evil, for being part of some of the most precious moments of my life 🤍✨
video_title: Resident Evil Veronica - Announcement Trailer

author: @NickTemperistic
text: 1:39:01 What a lot of people don't know is this isn't Telltale. It's the company that bought the bankrupt Telltale's assets that is making Wolf Among Us, and they also have the rights to make more Batman games too. Everything else went back to their original companies, which gave them the license. This new company is in Malibu, CA, and is outsourcing a lot of the work so they don't make the same mistakes. This will be the first Telltale game on the Unreal Engine, btw.

author: @thisisntme5742
text: People aren't willing to admit their own part to play in this. I've both been the blueberry trying to learn while low-man/speed runners beat every encounter without me learning a thing and the Sherpa wasting 6+ hours trying to teach people who won't pay attention or change what they're doing based on advice. Toxic players can be both experienced and inexperienced.
video_title: This Killed Raids.

author: @jonnyc212121
text:
He made a good point when he said that people who had raid armor , War raid armor. Let me tell you. Back in those days, having the full vog set was a major point of Pride even if it was on one character. Mostly because you could only get the helmet in hard mode . So having the full set on all three characters? That basically made you a god in the earlier days of d1.

There were only two things I never got from vog back then. Fatebringer and the mythoclast.
video_title: This Killed Raids.

author: @NachtBogen
text: I remember our fireteam struggling at the end of wrath on day 1.  We just kept wiping over and over again to the point where if we figured it wasn't going to happen one of us would say wipe so we can reset.  We had a good run going, but we were all just so tired.  We pushed aksis into final stand, and as we started my one friend jokingly said wipe.  To my horror we heard guardian down twice, and i had to yell god damnit don't wipe we got this.  We killed him as he was about to wipe us, and oh boy did we have words for our friend.  We laugh about it today at least
video_title: This Killed Raids.

author: @justpazzingby13
text:
There are currently 2 problems, and this applies to a lot of endgame content (in Destiny):
1. There's no reason to keep coming back to them.
2. There's no organic way to get into them.

The only solution I can think of is to open matchmaking for all of it. Anyone who wants to get into it will either find their people, or be frustrated enough to actually go and look for people.
video_title: This Killed Raids.

author: @SMERTRON426
text:
finally, A video I can relate.

I have been playing D2 since the red war campaign and raids were always something I wanted to try, unfortunately not a single friend of mine liked D2 and the only one that played with me, left after the vaulting,as of today since 2017-2018, I haven't got the chance to do a single raid ever, the only time I was close to finish one was Leviathan raid but 2 of the guardians got mad at another player (a new light player) for making too many mistakes and left the fireteam, thus leaving us incomplete for the raid.

there's always something: "hey do you have X expansion?", "Do you have X build?" "do you have X exotic or god roll?" "do you KWTD?" either I'm kicked or they abandon the fireteam, I HAVE TO PRACTICE TO LEARN , yes I've seen countless videos but if I never practice in a real raid I'm never gonna learn, and it doesn't help that they have little patience and that they want to rush everything.

Eventually I had to learn how to do everything solo, Legendaries, Master, GMs, dungeons because I never want to ask another player for help, Instead I'm the one helping others, I hope that after June 9th I can find a patient and understandable group of people because that's one of the experiences I want to live: to do a raid with people, any fkn raid, I'm in love with Vex Mythoclast but I can't reach it.
video_title: This Killed Raids.

author: @CryptoSteveB
text: The biggest problem with the last two Raid's in Destiny 2 is they became too mechanics heavy and too punishing when one or two players in the group screw up. Short time windows at an encounter to complete a trigonomics lesson is not what the average Destiny player wants to spend a weekend doing. The Day 1 streamers may of enjoyed the last two Destiny 2 Raid's but they had by far the lowest number of completions for everyone else.

author: @The-Local-Zombi
text:
A few things:
The broken tapes you unlock after you complete the full game are simply story games. You play as Clancy (the guy in the party room) for most of them and go through his night before he dies. Plus, you play as the baker family the night Mia and Evie show up. Next, there are two dlc’s for biohazard. You can play as Joe Baker and punch zombies while trying to save Zoe, and you can play as Chris (who is very important in RE8) and stop Lucas (if you really did want to fight him).

author: @eggfarf
text:
this was such a great playthrough, and the confirmation that u'll be following it up with re: village someday made it even better, max

also: earlier today, the resident evil: code veronica remake was officially announced at summer game fest! the announcement immediately made me hope that u'll consider playing it someday. before that, though, i'd love to see u play resident evil 2 remake, since it's the game that properly introduces claire redfield—the protagonist of re: code veronica and the sister of chris redfield, the same 'redfield' ethan meets at the end of re7 and the one we heard about at the end of requiem. after experiencing requiem—and having already played as leon—it would be so exciting to see u go back to where it all began (re2: remake)

i'm genuinely glad u decided to dive into resident evil, max. it's one of my favorite franchises, and ur playthroughs have been an absolute blast to watch. most of all, i'm excited to see the redfield siblings getting more attention again, especially claire with the upcoming code veronica remake. here's hoping u'll get to experience both re2 remake and code veronica someday!

author: @lucasurquiza554
text: I’ve wanted them to just make a Gundam game set in its own AU for a while now. Not constrained how things are made to work in ways that help move the plot forward, but rather free to design things in ways that benefit gameplay, and we finally got that. The decision to have… kaijus as the enemies was unexpected, but I can vibe with it. If this game is actually good and does well, it may just usher in a new golden age of Gundam gaming, and I couldn’t be more excited.
video_title: GUNDAM ROGUE ORBIT – Announcement Trailer

author: @hawgwild96
text:
Eric. Thank you for your service as always. I'm excited that there's new mechanics in gameplay such as the timing based catching, WR/DB jostles, additional analog tackling, the qb sneak meter. I notice in the QB sneak footage, you greened it but still didn't look like he got the 1st. I like that you may have had a successful jump but it's not a 100% that you're getting the first. The animations/physics look great for this and honestly separate itself from the rest of the game from an immersion standpoint.

 I wonder if this is their VERY early trial of introducing more physics-based play than animations as it has been for YEARS. I've always had nitpicks and eyesores when it comes to running the ball in the trenches. The run game has looked rough for years in the sense of realism. massive gaps for easy 10 yard runs inevitably brought down by a DB who shed a block.. or one DT sheds the line immediately (unrealistically) and makes the play behind the LOS.. etc.  There's no love for the LB's to rack up assisted tackles! lol but I'm glad they're showing new things that haven't been done before. Hopefully Tracab next year can finally bring us what we've waited for.
video_title: College Football 27 OFFICIAL Gameplay

author: @V-DaaR
text: As a 50-year-old gamer, normal difficulty often feels harder now, even though my reaction times are still probably above average. They’re just not what they were 20–30 years ago. I can still force my way through a soulslike, but it’s not very enjoyable , and eventually I won’t be able to do it at all. I don’t need «old people games,» but I do think accessibility options, difficulty sliders, and ways to reduce reaction-time pressure are going to matter more as lifelong gamers get older.

text: Regarding aging gamers, this is why the "get good" mentality annoys me and why I'm all for easy modes on games. As we age our reflexes slow down, so why are so many people against easy modes when we'll ALL eventually appreciate that it exists. I'm 40 and still appreciate higher difficulties, but I also have a degenerative genetic disorder that causes dislocations and pain in my hands and wrists, so I'm noticing limits now and have knocked a few games down to easy because I still want to experience them.

author: @silentfox-thewhitechaos8279
text: one would think, if you play on a new version of the game, that you would go and familiarize yourself with the new additions at least. Trial Chambers do indeed have unbreakable spawners, but once you have defeated a set number of spawned enemies from them, they deactivate. You reactivate them by drinking a potion of bad omen, which has taken the place of the original bad omen effect you gain from defeating banner pillagers. The box you thought was a spawner at the top when you first entered is a trial chest, you take keys that you get for beating trial spawners and use it on a trial chest and it gives you various rewards. orange keys open orange chests and you get those from normal spawners. Purple chests require purple keys, which you get from trial spawners under the bad omen effect.

author: @stickboystudios-u8e
text: This was actually an amazing video! I actually like the portrayal of actual justifiable vengeance, especially considering the Viewer's been through A LOT.  Ever since their first appearance in “Adopting YOU in Minecraft!”, they’ve been constantly mistreated by The Friend Group, with Jax, Dr. L, Emi, and Luna (all in my opinion) being the main factors of this. It felt especially delicious watching Emi get karma for her sin of anger because she’s been the most rude to the Viewer ever since they first showed up. Sure, they may have bonded during “YOU join our SLEEPOVER in Minecraft!” and “YOU become ANTI-GLITCHED in Minecraft!”, but remember that in “Adopting YOU in Minecraft!”, she tried to throw the Viewer (who just got there, mind you) into lava, tried to kill them with a Ravager, and tried to shoot the poor nonbinary person into space with a cannon, all in one video. Ultimately, it felt really nice seeing the Viewer retaliate after almost 2 months of this. Good work, Friend. Good work.
video_title: YOU turned EVIL in Minecraft!

text: Custom Adjustments looks like the foundation of something that could be really cool. Instead of a screen with one thousand menu options, they should allow you to make all the changes from a screen with play art. Let me see the play art and how it changes with each selection I make. author: @JeffD03
text: Been absolutely loving the MLB content! I'm sure someone has already told you, but for 3 inning games the stamina is reworked for pitchers which is why they get tired so quick. It forces you to use more than one so you can't just have one good pitcher in the whole time
video_title: I Played Draft Champions In MLB The Show!

author: @Fatfish33W
text: For your bullpen here are the “positions” which don’t technically matter but basically represent the order in which you would bring them in. LRP is long relief pitcher, this basically means a higher stamina guy who comes in if your starter gets knocked out early (he’s getting lit up and you need a guy to eat up innings so you don’t waste all your other pitchers) at the moment there isn’t a great option for that but they will eventually release guys like this. Last year it was usually a spotlight or topps now guy. MRP is middle reliever, this is kinda miscellaneous but essentially he would be the guy you would put in before your setup guy and closer. Usually in the 6th or 7th. Not usually your best guy but will get a clean inning or two. SU is setup pitcher. This is the guy you put in right before your closer. He compliments your closing pitcher by either throwing harder or slower and is usually the opposite hand as your closer to make your opponent adjust when your closer comes in. What it boils down to is that your setup is either your second best reliever or compliments your closer well. And obviously your closer is your best reliever who you bring in in the 9th. For you in wheel of DD Mason Miller. But the positions they list is more of guidelines than strict rules and you can kind of put whoever you want
video_title: I Played Draft Champions In MLB The Show!

author: @gulag_gary2125
text: MMG it is generally better to not use your diamond rounds on pitchers, it is best to figure out what mid tier relievers are solid and take them if possible and use your common and bronzes on starters. And then since it is just a 3 inning game you can use relievers instead of starting pitchers
video_title: I Played Draft Champions In MLB The Show!

author: @JeffSanders-v9d
text: Matt in general you want to have a fast contact guy batting first, and your best hitters or power guys batting 3rd and 4th. The silver and gold hitters should be lower down the order so they don’t have to bat as much.
